title: CEO & Operational Excellence Coach
required_skills: Minimum 5 years in VP/C-suite or equivalent senior leadership roles
Proven success driving operational strategies and leading high-performing teams
Demonstrated executive coaching, mentoring and leadership development expertise
Exceptional communication and influencing skills across all stakeholder levels
Strong business acumen — strategy, market dynamics and effective leadership practices
Bachelor degree in Business, Management or related field (advanced degree preferred)
bonus_skills: Experience managing complex operations with legal or compliance background
matched_skills: Programme governance and cross-functional coordination (HSBC, Hapag-Lloyd)
Operational process management (IPC: SOPs, training materials)
missing_skills: 5+ years VP/C-suite or equivalent senior leadership experience
Executive coaching and leadership development track record
Operational strategy design and implementation at organisational scale
Succession planning and talent development experience
core_responsibilities: Provide one-on-one executive coaching to senior leaders using structured coaching approach
Collaborate with senior management to design and implement operational efficiency strategies
Oversee key operational functions including financial management, HR and compliance
Serve as trusted advisor to CEO and executive team on leadership and organisational performance
Lead employee engagement, succession planning and high-performing culture initiatives
Foster business partnerships supporting internal excellence and external growth
recommended_emphasis: HSBC: Programme governance and C-suite stakeholder engagement — partial relevance to executive operations support
IPC: SOPs and training material development — minimal relevance to coaching mandate
summary: Boru scores 12.5 (poor match) for this executive coaching role that explicitly requires 5+ years of VP/C-suite leadership experience — a hard prerequisite Boru does not meet. The role is fundamentally designed for a seasoned executive transitioning into coaching, not a mid-career PM professional. Application is strongly not recommended.
